blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Who was it. The one doing the watching.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I couldn't tell you. It was dark. I was moving. I registered the pattern, not the face.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Couldn't say with certainty. The firelight doesn't do detail at that distance.
Davenport looked at him steadily. Evaluating. The look wasn't hostile — it was professional, the kind that reserves judgment until it has more to go on.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Crane. Julian Crane. I work in behavioral analysis. Consult, mostly. Fraud cases, corporate investigations. I notice patterns in how people behave and what those patterns mean.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Just a man who was walking the beach on a Friday night and noticed something.
Davenport took his hat off. Looked at the inside of it for a moment. Put it back on.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: The person being watched. Can you describe them?

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Taller than the others. Sitting at the outer edge of the group. The fire was to his right.
